title: Set docker_host environmental variable to rootless podman socket via podman-docker packet
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Please set the docker_host env variable and let it point to the rootless podman socket, now since podman officially supports rootless docker-compose.
Maybe via config file to choose between root and rootless socket.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Try to boot a docker-compose service without setting docker_host by yourself

**Describe the results you received:**

Cryptic permission denied errors.

**Describe the results you expected:**

My working services.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out!

Please reach out to your distribution if you want this solved in the podman-docker package.
The current podman-docker package should be setting up a symbolic link at boot time betweek /run/docker.sock->/run/podman/podman.sock

That's true
But what's the best way for the rootless way?
Filling the docker_host env in profile.d or giving an option in a settings file to redirect it to the rootless socket?

Yes profile.d is probably best.  Since mimicking the docker.sock in rootless mode, was never thought of.

Which socket does compose attempt to write to in rootless mode? Does it still try /var/run/docker.sock, or does it try a user specific socket?

Good to know

First it looks for docker_host, then it wants to use the root docker socket.
But maybe my informations are wrong.

Since docker also has a (for me not good working if I use networks) rootless mode, I wonder how docker-compose handles it.

But I think the socket can get changed here
https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/docker#Daemon_socket

And here it is further described I see now
https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/docker#Docker_rootless

https://docs.docker.com/engine/security/rootless/#install

maybe the podman docs need an update, since docker-compose rootless is now officially supported.
I never thinked before about looking in the docker rootless docs for the podman rootless usage.

It seems then docker-compose write to a rootless docker socket after following the docs.

Since docker can do it on the fly maybe a podman-docker could create this rootless docker.sock, too and redirect the stuff correctly.

When I look up rootless docker-compose guides, they don't do anything special to instruct compose to point to a different socket. This makes me think that compose is smart enough to know it's not running as root and use the rootless Docker socket, but this needs to be confirmed manually.

If this is true, perhaps it's enough to have a symlink from the Docker socket at `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/docker.sock` to Podman's non-root socket. I don't think we can do this in the `podman-docker` package automatically, but a single `ln -s` command is not a difficult addition to our rootless tutorial?
